channel.title: Interviews and Chats on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.
channel.description: Recent content in Interviews and Chats on Hi everyone. It's me, Tim.

item.title: Chats And Interviews
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I want to talk about chats and interviews.

And by that I mean chats on this channel with other people, when I have someone come on and we chat for an hour or two, or interviews on other people’s channels, where I appear on someone else’s channel or I do an interview with some other site. I want to talk about why I’m not doing any more of the former and very few of the latter. And I get asked this a lot. Um, it’s probably not the most frequent or even in the top five, but it’s certainly in the top 10 type of question I get: Hey Tim, you should be on this guy’s channel, or hey Tim, why don’t you bring this person on and talk about you know some game you made with them. And I love that.

item.title: Interview Questions
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today I want to talk about interview questions.

Stavos Nicolo asks: are there interview questions that you wish people asked you more often? That’s a good question. No one ever asks me meta questions like that — uh, not very often — where it’s like, instead of asking about a process or something, they’re asking a meta question about how the process goes, and my short answer is yes, there are questions that I wish wished people asked more often. There often things that I’ve done videos for on this channel — um, a big one, the biggest one that I often think is amusing is I rarely get asked about the team. I rarely get asked about people on the project.

item.title: Purpose Made Podcast
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. And today, instead of a normal video, I’m going to post a link below to an interview I did with Peter Bell on his purpose made podcast, where we talk about Fallout and games and the game industry and a whole bunch of other things for an hour and 45 minutes.

I hope you like it, enjoy, and I’ll be back with a regular video tomorrow.

item.title: A Chat With Scott Campbell
item.description: Tim, today I’m talking with Scott Campbell, the original lead designer, Fallout. We’re going to talk about Fallout things and what he’s been up to for the last forever. Yeah, seven years. Yeah, long long time, so you were the, you were one of the earliest team members on Fallout. You started on the team, I would think right after the two jasons. You know, uh, that was when I officially started. But uh, yeah, I mean since, like you know, even when we were just, like you know, playing gurps just for fun, after hours, you know I was, I was there, um, but even then, um, you know, I I have an actual trivia question for you, Tim. Do you remember the first time we met? Do you remember this?

item.title: A Chat With Jason Taylor
item.description:
Hi everyone. It’s me, Tim. Today I’m going to chat with Jason Taylor, JT, who was the first person on what would later be called team Fallout.

Hi, JT. Hey, how’s it going? Tim, I’m good. How are you? Great? It has been a long time you came to see the talk at GDC. Yeah, like 10 years ago.

You were in town and I was — I believe I was working in San Francisco at the time, uh — and then we met up and chatted. I saw your presentation was good, we had lunch afterwards, but I remember I didn’t know you’re going to be there and you were in the front and I was — like I have to stay honest now because he’s right there — like, yeah, I don’t remember you saying anything that made me want to jump out of my chair. So that’s why I told Leonard — this is why I like doing it, because I have my notes and I have everything. But a lot of this is perspective based and I find that sometimes I’ll leave a meeting with someone and we have a very different take on what someone literally just said. So, as, the person who started right away in the very like I got started in early 94 and then in Fall 94, you and Jason Anderson started I think the same week, but I think you actually started before him and you and I shared an office, yep, so I kind of wanted you to talk about very early. It was team gurps back then.

item.title: A Chat With Leonard Boyarsky, Part 2
item.description:
Him and I’m joined again today for part two of my chat with Leonard bararski. Hey Leonard, hey Tim, how’s it going? I’m good.

Before we get started, I saw that you, uh, showed your gnod trophy. I wanted to show my Award of Excellence that interplay gave me. Um, the funny thing about this is I think Jason got one too. There was a party — there’s a party — where all of interplay was at this party and they gave out that award. I don’t know if this is what happened. I think this is what happened with Gana too. You don’t remember what I’m gonna say, do you? So the next day, Rob comes in and he gives it to me. I’m like what is this?

item.title: A Chat With Leonard Boyarsky
item.description: I have my first guest, a man who needs no introduction: no, it’s Leonard burarski. I’ve known him for 30 years. We’ve been working together for much of that time — not all of it. He had his own Adventures and he looks exactly the same, and I look like a picture of Dorian Gray, so that is. That is verifiably untrue. People can go Google and make their own decision.
